author: stardust
description: Team NPC separation plugin with map partition-based housing system - Red and Blue teams each have independent town NPCs with official Terraria housing validation
purpose: Implements a custom NPC faction system where Red team (team ID 1) and Blue team (team ID 3) each have their own separate instances of all town NPCs. Each team must independently meet the arrival conditions for their NPCs. Version 2.0 adds map-based partitioning (left/right split) with comprehensive housing validation and persistence.
key_features: Map-based partitioning: Left side (x <= Main.spawnTileX) = Red team, Right side (x > Main.spawnTileX) = Blue team
Official Terraria housing validation: Room size (60-750 tiles), furniture requirements, background walls
Spiral search algorithm for efficient house finding within partition boundaries
Housing assignment persistence: Survives server restarts via JSON storage
Separate NPC instances for Red and Blue teams
Independent arrival condition tracking per team
Prevents vanilla NPC spawning - plugin manages all town NPC spawning
Team-colored arrival messages (red for Red team, blue for Blue team)
MoveNPC restriction - only same-team players can move NPCs
No dialogue restriction - all players can talk to any NPC
Automatic NPC respawning when conditions are met
Configurable spawn centers and check intervals

components.purpose: Tracks team state including players, money, items collected, biomes visited, and NPC count. NEW in v2.0: Partition boundaries (MinX/MaxX), spiral search algorithm, house assignment tracking with HouseAssignment objects.
components.v2_additions: MinX/MaxX properties, HousingValidator reference, GenerateSpiralPattern(), IsHouseOccupied(), AssignHouse(), ReleaseHouse(), GetHouseAssignments()

components.purpose: Manages NPC-to-team mappings using Dictionary<int, string> for NPC index to team, and Dictionary<(string, string), List<int>> for tracking NPCs per team.
JSON configuration management using System.Text.Json. Stores spawn centers, check intervals, and plugin settings. NEW in v2.0: Partition settings, housing validation options, persistence settings.
components.v2_additions: PartitionBoundary, EnableStrictHousingValidation, MinHouseDistance, EnableHousingPersistence, AutoSaveIntervalSeconds, SpiralSearchRadius

components.purpose: Defines spawn rules for 25+ town NPCs with Func<TeamState, bool> conditions and NPCID mappings.
Handles NPC spawning via NPC.NewNPC(), blocks vanilla spawns via OnNpcSpawn event, sets NPC homes, and manages town NPC lifecycle.

Checks spawn conditions every 10 seconds (configurable). Validates 4 required conditions: daytime, no special events, NPC-specific conditions, and valid unoccupied housing. NEW in v2.0: Auto-save functionality, persistence saving after NPC spawn/death.
components.v2_additions: autoSaveTicks counter, config reference, auto-save logic in OnGameUpdate(), HousingPersistence.Save() calls

components.purpose: NEW in v2.0: Implements official Terraria housing validation standards. Validates room size (60-750 tiles), checks required furniture (light source, flat surface, comfort item), verifies player-placed background walls, calculates home tile scores.
components.key_methods: ValidateHousing(), CheckRoomWithWorldGen(), CheckRoomSize(), CheckRequiredFurniture(), HasLightSource(), HasFlatSurface(), HasComfortItem(), CheckBackgroundWalls(), FindBestHomeTile(), CalculateHomeTileScore()

components.purpose: NEW in v2.0: Manages JSON persistence of housing assignments. Saves/loads housing data to tshock/teamnpc_housing.json, revalidates houses on load, handles server restarts gracefully.

spawn_conditions.description: NPCs spawn only when ALL 4 conditions are met

conditions.name: No Special Events
conditions.check: No invasion, eclipse, blood moon, pumpkin moon, snow moon, slime rain, or rain
conditions.implementation: IsSpecialEvent() method checks Main.invasionType, Main.eclipse, Main.bloodMoon, Main.pumpkinMoon, Main.snowMoon, Main.slimeRain, Main.raining

conditions.name: NPC-Specific Conditions
conditions.check: Each NPC has unique spawn requirements defined in TownNPCDefinitions.TownNpcSpawnRules

conditions.name: Valid Unoccupied Housing
conditions.check: Must have a valid house that is not occupied by another NPC, within team's partition boundaries
conditions.implementation_v2: HousingValidator.ValidateHousing() performs comprehensive checks: (1) WorldGen.StartRoomCheck() for basic room structure, (2) Room size 60-750 tiles, (3) Required furniture detection (light source, flat surface, comfort item), (4) Player-placed background walls only, (5) Home tile scoring system (base 50, solid tile -5, empty +5, closed door -20, must have 3 tiles standing room)
conditions.partition_constraint: Spiral search only within MinX to MaxX boundaries, left partition (x <= Main.spawnTileX) for Red team, right partition (x > Main.spawnTileX) for Blue team
conditions.search_algorithm: GenerateSpiralPattern() creates outward spiral from spawn center (150 tile radius default), checking each point for validity and occupation status

v2_new_settings.PartitionBoundary: -1 means use Main.spawnTileX automatically, or set specific X coordinate
v2_new_settings.EnableStrictHousingValidation: When true, uses full Terraria housing validation (furniture, walls, room size)
v2_new_settings.MinHouseDistance: Minimum distance in tiles between NPC houses (occupation check radius)
v2_new_settings.EnableHousingPersistence: Save/load housing assignments to JSON file
v2_new_settings.AutoSaveIntervalSeconds: How often to auto-save housing data (300 = 5 minutes)
v2_new_settings.SpiralSearchRadius: Maximum search radius from spawn center for valid houses

terraria_apis: Main.tile[x, y] - ITile interface with .active() and .type properties
Main.npc[] - Array of all NPCs in the world
NPC.downedBoss1, NPC.downedGoblins, etc. - Boss defeat flags
WorldGen.StartRoomCheck(x, y) - NEW v2.0: Validates room structure, sets WorldGen.roomX1/Y1/X2/Y2 boundaries
WorldGen.roomX1/Y1/X2/Y2 - Room boundary coordinates after StartRoomCheck()
Main.spawnTileX - World spawn point X coordinate, used as partition boundary
Main.tileSolid[type] - Check if tile type is solid
TileID.* constants - Tile type identifiers (Torches, Chairs, Tables, etc.)
WallID.* constants - Wall type identifiers for natural wall detection
tshock_apis: GetDataHandlers.NPCHome - NPC home change packet handler
TSPlayer.All.SendData() - Syncs data to all clients

event_handling.OnPostInit: Clears existing town NPCs if ClearNPCsOnLoad is enabled
event_handling.OnGameUpdate: Delegates to ArrivalConditionChecker.OnGameUpdate() for periodic condition checking
event_handling.OnNpcSpawn: Blocks vanilla town NPC spawning by setting args.Handled = true and npc.active = false
event_handling.OnNpcKilled: Unregisters NPC from team manager, allows automatic respawn if conditions still met
event_handling.OnUpdateNPCHome: Prevents cross-team NPC movement by checking player.TPlayer.team against npc team, restores original position if denied

important_implementation_details.npc_registration: When spawning, NPC index is registered with NPCTeamManager.RegisterNPC(index, npcName, teamName)
important_implementation_details.arrival_messages: Only sent when npcIndex >= 0 (spawn succeeded), uses team-colored messages via Color.Red or Color.Blue
important_implementation_details.housing_validation_v2: HousingValidator performs multi-step validation: (1) WorldGen.StartRoomCheck() for structure, (2) Size check 60-750 tiles, (3) Furniture detection via HashSet lookups, (4) Background wall validation against NaturalWalls list, (5) Home tile scoring with standing room check
important_implementation_details.partition_boundaries: Red team MinX=0 MaxX=partitionBoundary, Blue team MinX=partitionBoundary+1 MaxX=Main.maxTilesX, boundary defaults to Main.spawnTileX
important_implementation_details.spiral_search: GenerateSpiralPattern() creates square spiral: top row left-to-right, right column top-to-bottom, bottom row right-to-left, left column bottom-to-top, expanding outward 1 tile per loop
important_implementation_details.house_occupation: IsHouseOccupied() checks if any NPC within 5 tiles (configurable via MinHouseDistance), tracks via npcHouseAssignments dictionary
important_implementation_details.persistence_flow: Save triggered on: (1) Plugin Dispose, (2) NPC spawn, (3) NPC death, (4) Auto-save timer (every 5 min). Load on plugin Initialize, revalidates all houses with HousingValidator
important_implementation_details.tile_api: Uses ITile interface: tile.active() to check if active, tile.type for tile type, tile.wall for wall type, Main.tileSolid[] for solid check. IMPORTANT: Use 'var' instead of 'Tile' type to avoid ITile/Tile conversion errors
important_implementation_details.spawn_timing: Checks every 10 seconds (600 ticks at 60fps) by default, configurable via CheckIntervalSeconds
important_implementation_details.npc_home_setting: Sets homeTileX, homeTileY, homeless=false, then syncs with PacketTypes.UpdateNPCHome
important_implementation_details.house_preservation: On NPC death, house assignment is NOT released - preserved for respawn. House only released manually or if validation fails on reload

issues_fixed_during_development.solution: Simplified housing validation to manually check for occupied houses

issues_fixed_during_development.issue: Arrival messages sent but NPCs not spawning

issues_fixed_during_development.solution: Changed from tile.HasTile to tile.active() and tile.TileType to tile.type

issues_fixed_during_development.issue: TileID constants not found (Tiki, SkullLantern, HeartLantern, etc.)
issues_fixed_during_development.solution: Removed non-existent TileID constants from light source list, kept only compatible tile types

issues_fixed_during_development.issue: WallID constants not found (WoodUnsafe, GrayBrickUnsafe, RedBrickUnsafe)
issues_fixed_during_development.solution: Removed non-existent WallID constants from natural walls list

issues_fixed_during_development.issue: Cannot implicitly convert ITile to Tile errors
issues_fixed_during_development.solution: Changed all 'Tile tile' declarations to 'var tile' to use ITile interface

installation: 1. Build the plugin: cd ServerPlugins/TeamNPCPlugin && dotnet build
2. Copy TeamNPCPlugin.dll from bin/Debug/net9.0/ to TShock ServerPlugins folder
4. Configuration file will be auto-generated at tshock/teamnpc.json
configuration_steps: 1. Edit tshock/teamnpc.json to set spawn centers for Red and Blue teams
2. RedTeamSpawnX/Y should be near Red team base
3. BlueTeamSpawnX/Y should be near Blue team base
4. HouseSearchRadius determines how far from spawn center to search for valid houses
5. Set EnableDebugLog to true for detailed logging
usage_instructions.team_assignment: Players must join teams using Terraria's built-in team system (T key) - Red team (1) or Blue team (3)

development_workflow.description: After each code modification, follow these steps to build and deploy
steps: 2. Copy DLL: Copy TeamNPCPlugin.dll from bin/Debug/net9.0/ to TShockLauncher/bin/Debug/net9.0/ServerPlugins
3. Restart TShock server to load the updated plugin

major_changes: Map-based partitioning: World split at Main.spawnTileX into left (Red) and right (Blue) zones
Official Terraria housing validation using WorldGen.StartRoomCheck()
Comprehensive furniture detection: light sources, flat surfaces, comfort items
Background wall validation to ensure player-placed walls only
Spiral search algorithm for efficient house finding (150 tile radius)
Housing assignment persistence with JSON storage (tshock/teamnpc_housing.json)
House preservation on NPC death for respawning
files_modified: TeamState.cs (+110 lines) - Partition boundaries, spiral search, house tracking
TeamNPCPlugin.cs (+50 lines) - Component integration, persistence load/save

notes: This plugin is completely independent from other TShock plugins
Plugin manages ALL town NPC spawning - vanilla spawning is disabled
Each team needs to independently meet conditions for their NPCs
NPCs are tied to their team and cannot be moved by other teams
Housing must be built near the configured spawn centers
Conditions are checked every 10 seconds by default
NEW v2.0: Houses must meet official Terraria standards (furniture, walls, size)
NEW v2.0: NPCs will only search for houses within their partition boundary
NEW v2.0: Housing assignments persist across server restarts
NEW v2.0: Spiral search is more efficient than random search (v1.0)
Houses are validated on load - invalid houses are automatically cleared
